name: Apache Struts 2 Remote Code Execution Detected (CVE-2017-5638)
description: Detects a possible exploitation of the CVE-2017-5638 vulnerability in Apache Struts 2, which allows for remote code execution via manipulated HTTP Content-Type headers. The rule looks for specific patterns in HTTP traffic that are indicative of an OGNL injection attempt.

prompts.prompt: A critical alert has been triggered: a possible exploitation attempt for **CVE-2017-5638** (Apache Struts 2 RCE) has been detected on the server **{computer}**. The HTTP request originated from the source IP **{src_ip}** and targeted the URL **{url}**. The malicious payload was identified in the `Content-Type` header, which contained the value **{content_type}**. This is a strong indicator of an **Initial Access** attack using the **Exploit Public-Facing Application** technique (**MITRE ATT&CK T1190**). Immediate action is required to block the source IP, isolate the server, and investigate for any post-exploitation activity. Verify that the Apache Struts 2 instance is patched to a non-vulnerable version.

parameters.name: src_ip
parameters.description: The source IP address of the malicious request.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL targeted by the request.

parameters.name: content_type
parameters.description: The value of the HTTP Content-Type header containing the malicious payload.

parameters.name: computer
parameters.description: The name of the server where the event occurred.
